document.title: Risk Management Report for B1417MCW / B1717MCW / FB1417MCW / FB1717MCW

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models B1417MCW, B1717MCW, FB1417MCW, and FB1717MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents risk management activities, risk acceptability criteria, residual risk evaluation, and production/post-production information collection for the listed medical device models.
keyPoints: The device is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector with classification IIa and expected service life of approximately 5 years.
Applicable national and regional regulations listed are Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea.
The report applies EN ISO 14971:2019 risk management criteria with acceptable risk levels 1-4 and unacceptable risk levels 5-25.
Before risk control, 43 risks were identified, with 2 acceptable and 41 unacceptable.
After risk control, all 43 identified residual risks were evaluated in the acceptable region.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: This is a document for the H&abyz Co., Ltd. to establish and document a risk management plan in accordance with the risk management procedure.

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

entities.name: European Union
entities.evidence: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017

entities.name: Republic of Korea
entities.evidence: Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea

concepts.name: Risk management
concepts.evidence: risk management means systematic application of management policies, procedures (3.13) and practices to the tasks of analysing, evaluating, controlling and monitoring risk

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: risk management file set of records (3.16) and other documents that are produced by risk management

concepts.name: Residual risk
concepts.evidence: residual risk risk remaining after risk control (3.21) measures have been implemented

concepts.name: Risk acceptability criteria
concepts.evidence: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4 Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance
concepts.evidence: Refer to A-TCF-10-161 Post-Market Surveillance for getting the information of production and post- production phases.

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance.

evidence.claim: The document is Rev.0 and was established on Oct 18, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision No. 0 Rev. Date Oct/18/2024 Revision Description Establishment

evidence.claim: The listed product models are B1417MCW, B1717MCW, FB1417MCW, and FB1717MCW.
evidence.pageTarget: B1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The product is classified as IIa.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Classification

evidence.claim: The expected service life is approximately 5 years.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Service Life

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is a digital imaging solution for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The B1417MCW/B1717MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Purpose

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography, infants, pregnant women, fluoroscopy, or angiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women. Not to be used for fluoroscopy and angiography.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Contraindications

evidence.claim: Applicable national and regional regulations listed are EU Regulation 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017 Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea

evidence.claim: The report references EN ISO 14971:2019 for medical device risk management.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2019 Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices (ISO 14971:2019)

evidence.claim: Before risk control, 43 risks were identified and estimated.
evidence.evidenceText: Through the identification of known and foreseeable hazards, 43 risks were identified and estimated.

evidence.claim: Before risk control, 2 risks were acceptable and 41 risks were unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: The risk evaluation results are determined that; - Acceptable region: 2 ea - Unacceptable region: 41 ea

evidence.claim: After risk control, all 43 identified residual risks were in the acceptable region.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in an acceptable region.

evidence.claim: The overall residual risks were determined to be acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Finally, the overall residual risks are determined to be acceptable through the warning, operating method and adverse event reporting.

evidence.claim: Production and post-production information collection includes customer satisfaction surveys, complaint statistics, inspection and test monitoring, measuring device monitoring, design changes, standards monitoring, market information, process monitoring, and purchaser assessment monitoring.
evidence.evidenceText: Department Information collecting and review in production and post-production Quality Assurance 1. Customer satisfaction survey 2. Statistical processing of customer complaints 3. Monitoring results of the incoming inspection and end product test results 4. Monitoring the measuring devices R&D 1. Design changes 2. Monitoring the national and international standards 3. Collect and analysis of the market information Production Process management monitoring Management Monitoring the purchaser assessment results

evidence.claim: The Quality manager is responsible for collecting and reviewing production and post-production information and managing quality problems.
evidence.evidenceText: Responsibility and the role of the Quality manager Collect, review the information and manage the quality problems in production and post-production phase

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text lists European Union and Republic of Korea regulations and does not mention FDA.
The document states risk management activities were performed and completed, but also says production and post-production related activities are excluded.
Source text contains apparent typographical/OCR issues in some standard references and wording, including EN 15322-1 where EN 15223-1 may have been intended.
